items.text:
Quigley,
I'm standing on a platform
sized for five abreast.

It's gilded, and engraved
at such a level of detail
as to feel just wholly extravagant.

Most of the detail resides underfoot.
Why make nice carvings
that will abrade with each step?

But it has me thinking.
Many things here are not what they seem.

For one who can build
an overworld such as this,

who can reproduce these
buildings, objects, natural environments
at such scale and breadth --

surely for him, conjuring a little gold
isn't a problem.

Who would consider gold to be precious
when it is as easy to acquire as dirt?

Yet I'm impressed, and maybe that's it:
to show ignorant visitors
that these golden areas are special.

I've caught glimpses of several such platforms --
perhaps there's a pattern to their distribution --

but this is the first I have managed to reach,
and I've yet to touch its central glyph.

So I've no idea why it's important,
but it's time to find out.
